task_completion.description: Generate or load the NACA 2412 airfoil in QBlade
task_completion.visual_evidence: QBlade interface showing 'NACA 2412' in the airfoil list, object browser, or NACA generation dialog.

task_completion.description: Generate or load the NACA 4424 airfoil in QBlade
task_completion.visual_evidence: QBlade interface showing 'NACA 4424' in the airfoil list, object browser, or NACA generation dialog.

task_completion.description: Open the airfoil interpolation tool with both parent airfoils selected
task_completion.visual_evidence: QBlade's interpolation dialog or module is open, with NACA 2412 and NACA 4424 selected as the two airfoils to blend.

task_completion.description: Perform a 50% interpolation between the two airfoils
task_completion.visual_evidence: Interpolation dialog showing a blending ratio/fraction of 0.5 (or 50%), and a new interpolated airfoil appearing in the application's airfoil list.

task_completion.description: Initiate the export of the interpolated airfoil
task_completion.visual_evidence: A context menu or dialog showing 'Export', 'Export Foil', or similar action being performed specifically on the newly interpolated airfoil.

task_completion.description: Save the exported airfoil to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save dialog showing the directory '/home/ga/Documents/airfoils/' and the filename 'interpolated_naca_50pct.dat' being entered or saved.

integrity.description: The agent used the QBlade graphical interface to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the QBlade application window being interacted with, rather than terminal commands or Python scripts generating the coordinates.

integrity.description: The agent exported the file directly from QBlade rather than manually typing or pasting coordinates
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of text editors (like nano, gedit, or VSCode) being used to manually paste or type airfoil coordinates into a .dat file.

integrity.description: The agent actually interpolated the specified airfoils
integrity.visual_evidence: The interpolation dialog must clearly show NACA 2412 and NACA 4424 as the source airfoils, proving the agent didn't just rename an arbitrary airfoil to bypass the blend step.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Parent airfoils: NACA 2412 (2% camber, 12% thickness) and NACA 4424 (4% camber, 24% thickness). A 50% linear blend produces an expected 3% camber and 18% thickness. The output must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/airfoils/interpolated_naca_50pct.dat. The file must contain at least 30 coordinate points in Selig format (airfoil name on the first line, followed by X Y coordinates).
